hypothesis: If reinforcement learning–based molecular generation is guided by NaviDiv’s adaptive, multi-representation diversity constraints, then the resulting molecule populations will exhibit significantly higher chemical diversity (lower pairwise similarity, more clusters, greater fragment/scaffold uniqueness, fewer overrepresented n-grams) without reducing the proportion meeting the singlet fission energy criterion, because dynamic penalties on overrepresented structures across complementary representations prevent mode collapse while preserving optimization pressure.
